Priority Connection Angles

Ranked by strength of connection. Each entry shows the verified mechanism, recommended engagement angle, and primary source. Click to expand.

Research Methodology

Sources consulted: Companies House, BritishAmerican Business website, Vodafone Annual Report 2025, GC Powerlist UK (Legal500), Vodafone UK News Centre, WPP corporate filings, BITC executive biographies, Copenhagen Airport IR, The Org, Bloomberg, Financial Times. The 100 Group membership confirmed via BITC biography (Echave) and grokipedia/Bocconi sources (Della Valle). Virgin Media GC tenure confirmed via The Org, Global Legal Post, and The In-House Lawyer. All source links verified March 2026. Prepared by Differentiated.

Open Research Gaps

Seven research gaps remain open. Helen Elsby is the highest priority — as Chief Solutions Officer and Acting CDIO, she controls Heathrow's entire technology estate, yet no verified connection to Vodafone leadership has been identified.

Source References

All sources consulted during research. Every connection claim is linked to its primary source. Verified March 2026.

Connection #1 — Echave ↔ Della Valle · The 100 Group
1.1
BITC — Javier Echave biography
Explicitly states: "He is also an executive mentor, member of The 100 Group." Primary source for Echave's membership 2017–2024.
1.2
Bocconi University — Alumna of the Year 2022 announcement
Confirms Della Valle "has been elected Chairman of 100 Group, the association that brings together FTSE 100 CFOs." Primary source for chairmanship.
1.3
Grokipedia — Margherita Della Valle profile
Confirms Della Valle "chaired the 100 Group from 2022 until 2025." Source for chairmanship end date.
Connection #2 — Hifzi ↔ Vodafone UK · MVNO Deal
2.1
Vodafone UK News Centre — Virgin Media MVNO agreement press release
Official Vodafone announcement of the MVNO deal, 6 November 2019. Primary source for deal date and commercial terms.
2.2
Liberty Global — Virgin Media MVNO agreement press release
Confirms deal signed 6 November 2019, five-year term to 2026, full MVNO with voice and data.
2.3
The Org — Mine Hifzi profile (Heathrow)
Confirms Hifzi at Virgin Media "from February 2014 to July 2022" — she was GC throughout the deal signing (Nov 2019) and the 5G launch (Jan 2021).
2.4
The In-House Lawyer — Significant Matters, Winter 2022
Confirms Hifzi joined Heathrow in July 2022 directly from Virgin Media. Corroborating source for tenure dates.
2.5
Global Legal Post — Heathrow secures GC from Virgin Media
Announces Hifzi appointment from Virgin Media to Heathrow. Describes her as having "almost eight years at Virgin Media." Published December 2021.
Connection #3 — Jansen ↔ Dingemans · WPP Board
3.1
WPP plc — Philip Jansen appointment as Non-Executive Chair
Official WPP announcement, July 2024. Confirms Jansen joined board 16 September 2024, became Chair 1 January 2025.
3.2
Heathrow Media Centre — Philip Jansen appointed Chairman
Official Heathrow press release confirming Jansen as Non-Executive Chairman from 1 January 2026, succeeding Lord Paul Deighton.
3.3
WPP plc — The WPP Board (current)
Live board listing confirming both Jansen (Chair) and Dingemans (NED) as current WPP directors.
3.4
Wikipedia — Philip Jansen
Confirms BT Group CEO February 2019–January 2024; WPP Chair from September 2024.
Connection #4 — Echave ↔ Dingemans · The 100 Group
5.1
BITC — Javier Echave biography
Confirms Echave as member of The 100 Group. See also 1.1 above.
5.2
GSK — Board of Directors (archived)
GSK biography for Dingemans confirms his chairmanship of The 100 Group 2014–2016 during his tenure as GSK CFO.
Connection #5 — Hifzi ↔ De Bie · GC Powerlist
6.1
Legal 500 — GC Powerlist UK 2019
Both Hifzi (Virgin Media) and de Bie (Royal Mail) appear in the 2019 edition. Small annual reception for all honorees.
6.2
Legal 500 — GC Powerlist UK 2023
Hifzi featured again as Heathrow GC. Confirms continued presence in senior in-house legal community.
Connection #6 — Woldbye ↔ De Bie · easyJet / Copenhagen Airport
7.1
Copenhagen Airport IR
Confirms Woldbye as CEO of Copenhagen Airport 2011–2023.
7.2
Legal 500 — GC Powerlist UK 2020
Confirms de Bie at easyJet from 2019. easyJet operated 10–11 routes from Copenhagen during the overlap period.
7.3
Vodafone Annual Report 2025
Confirms de Bie as Group General Counsel & Company Secretary, Vodafone Group.
Connection #7 — Woldbye / Milton ↔ Gliddon · CBI / BritishAmerican Business
8.1
BritishAmerican Business — International Advisory Board
Confirms Woldbye's membership of the BAB International Advisory Board. Vodafone confirmed as BAB corporate member.
8.2
Qwoted — Nick Gliddon, CBI Annual Conference 2021
Confirms Gliddon as speaker at CBI Annual Conference 2021.
Connection #8 — Jansen ↔ Taylor · BT / EE
9.1
VodafoneThree — Max Taylor biography
Confirms Taylor's MD Commercial & Marketing role at EE/BT prior to joining Vodafone UK.
9.2
Wikipedia — Philip Jansen
Confirms Jansen became BT Group CEO February 2019. Brief structural overlap with Taylor in early 2019.
General / Executive Profiles
G.1
Heathrow.com — Executive Committee (official)
Primary source for all Heathrow executive titles, tenures, and official biographies.
G.2
Heathrow.com — The Board (official)
Confirms Philip Jansen as Non-Executive Chairman from 1 January 2026.
G.3
Companies House — UK company officer search
Used to verify directorship appointments for all Heathrow executives. Hifzi: 315 Virgin Media group appointments (bulk March 2014). Milton filed as MILTON-TOMKINS.
G.4
Broadband TV News — Virgin Media strikes 5G mobile deal with Vodafone
Trade press coverage of the November 2019 MVNO deal. Confirms deal date, five-year term, and 5G launch timeline.
G.5
Wikipedia — Virgin Mobile (UK)
Confirms Virgin Mobile launched on Vodafone network January 2021. Corroborates MVNO deal timeline.
G.6
The Org — Mine Hifzi profile (Virgin Media)
Confirms Hifzi "appointed General Counsel at Virgin Media in 2014 and since October 2019 is also responsible for government, regional and regulatory affairs."
Methodology Note

Research was conducted entirely from public sources. No LinkedIn premium data, paid databases (BoardEx, Refinitiv), or proprietary tools were used. Sources consulted include: corporate press releases, official company biography pages, Companies House officer records, trade press, legal industry publications (Legal 500 GC Powerlist), university alumni communications, and Wikipedia. All source URLs were live-checked in March 2026. The 100 Group does not publish its membership list publicly — membership was confirmed via BITC executive biography (Echave) and Bocconi/Grokipedia sources (Della Valle). Where verification required inference rather than primary source, this is noted in the relevant connection card.
